title_en: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
title_zh: 特朗普对加拿大进口商品征收50%关税
summary_zh: 美国总统特朗普宣布对加拿大进口商品征收50%关税，以报复美方所称汽车、乳制品和酒类遭到的“不公平待遇”。新关税覆盖部分消费品和工业品，但能源、钾肥等被豁免，定于30天后生效。此举加剧了北美贸易紧张局势，相关行政令未提及野火烟雾，而是列出此前已知的贸易摩擦点，意味着双边谈判破裂。美方依据1930年关税法第338条征税，此前最高法院曾否决其使用紧急权力征收关税。加拿大商界呼吁在关税生效前取得谈判进展。美国未续签现行美墨加协定，改为逐年滚动审查。
reading_passage:
US President Donald Trump has imposed a 50% tariff on a wide range of goods imported from Canada, in retaliation for what he called "unequal treatment" of US cars, dairy and alcohol.

The duties take effect within 30 days and mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

The White House said the tariffs were necessary to protect American businesses.

Goods targeted range from everyday consumer items like wine and hockey sticks to industrial goods such as commercial cement. However, several key exports will be spared, such as energy, potash, critical minerals and fish.

The new duties apply to all covered goods regardless of whether the product was included under the existing free trade agreement between Canada, the US and Mexico, known as the USMCA.

Monday's import taxes build on trade barriers already in place between the two nations.

The US has been maintaining active tariffs ranging from 15% to 50% on Canadian steel, aluminum and copper. Washington also charges a 35% tariff on Canadian softwood lumber, alongside a 25% tax on non-US parts in cars.

Canada has its own 25% counter-tariff on selected imports of American steel, aluminium and vehicles.

Monday's duties come in the wake of President Trump's threat to impose tariffs over Canadian wildfire smoke drifting into US cities.

But there is no mention of wildfires in the executive orders that Trump signed on Monday.

Instead, the three proclamations list US trade irritants that were previously known to Canada related to cars, dairy and alcohol - signalling a breakdown of trade negotiations between the two countries.

On cars, Trump is accusing Canada of charging a tax on US motor vehicles and parts that are not covered under USMCA.

He argues it is "unreasonable" and that Canada has discriminated against the US by not charging other countries a similar tax.

Automotive manufacturing in North America is highly integrated between Canada, the US and Mexico.

But Trump's Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has said in the past that he believes Canada should "come second" to the US.

Trump has also named cars in the past as one issue where the two countries have competing interests.

Dairy, meanwhile, has long been a problem for the US, specifically Canada's supply management system, which sets limits on foreign imports. Those that exceed the limit are charged a tariff upwards of 300%.

And lastly, the enduring boycott of US booze by most Canadian provinces has become a major sore point for the Americans since it was imposed last year.

Canadian premiers have said repeatedly that the boycott will be lifted if the US removes its tariffs on key Canadian sectors, including metals and automobiles.

Canadian trade negotiators have been working on trying to secure a deal that would at least reduce some of the current US tariffs.

The BBC has contacted the White House and the Canadian government for comment.

In February, the US Supreme Court struck down sweeping international tariffs imposed by Trump through the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977.

The justices ruled that the president had exceeded his authority when he announced the duties under a law reserved for national emergencies.

The White House vowed at the time that it would invoke other mechanisms to impose import taxes.

Monday's duties have been introduced under Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act, which covers trade discrimination rather than national emergencies.

Candance Laing, head of the Canadian Chamber of Commerce, called the move a "regrettable decision", but urged officials to make "meaningful progress" in talks before the new duties take effect in 30 days' time.

Chris Swonger, head of the Distilled Spirits Council of the United States, also called for both sides to find a solution to their dispute, warning the decision "deepens trade tensions and raises the risk of further retaliation".

Earlier this year, the US chose not to renew the USMCA in its current form.

Canada and Mexico have requested a renewal of the pact, but the US wants to make changes to the deal, which was negotiated during Trump's first term in office.

As it stands, the treaty will continue to govern North American trade over the next decade on a rolling, year-to-year basis, requiring annual reviews.

vocabulary.example: The new import duties on steel are expected to protect domestic producers.
The escalation of the conflict worried international observers.
The company's decision was made in retaliation for the competitor's aggressive pricing.
The government decided to impose higher taxes on luxury goods.
The President signed an executive order to establish a new climate task force.
The two countries are engaged in trade negotiations to reduce tariffs.
The supply management system in agriculture controls production quotas to stabilize prices.
The court struck down the controversial legislation on constitutional grounds.
The new policy will take effect next month.
The treaty includes a mechanism for resolving trade disputes.

difficult_sentences.sentence: The new duties apply to all covered goods regardless of whether the product was included under the existing free trade agreement between Canada, the US and Mexico, known as the USMCA.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: 本句的主干为 `The new duties apply to all covered goods`，`regardless of whether...` 为让步状语从句，表示“无论……”，`known as the USMCA` 为过去分词短语作同位语，说明该协定的名称。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 让步状语从句 + 同位语
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 新关税适用于所有受覆盖的商品，无论该产品是否已纳入现有的美墨加自由贸易协定（即USMCA）。

difficult_sentences.sentence: In February, the US Supreme Court struck down sweeping international tariffs imposed by Trump through the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: `imposed by Trump` 为过去分词短语作后置定语，修饰 `tariffs`；`through the ... Act` 为介词短语说明征收关税所依据的法律。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 过去分词作后置定语 + 介词短语说明依据
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 今年二月，美国最高法院废除了特朗普依据1977年《国际紧急经济权力法》征收的大范围国际关税。

difficult_sentences.sentence: Candance Laing, head of the Canadian Chamber of Commerce, called the move a 'regrettable decision', but urged officials to make 'meaningful progress' in talks before the new duties take effect in 30 days' time.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: `head of ...` 为同位语，指明主语身份；`called ... but urged ...` 为并列谓语结构；`before the new duties take effect ...` 为时间状语从句。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 同位语 + 并列谓语 + 时间状语从句
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 加拿大商会总裁坎迪斯·莱恩称此举是个“令人遗憾的决定”，但敦促官员在新关税30天后生效前通过谈判取得“有意义的进展”。

difficult_sentences.sentence: As it stands, the treaty will continue to govern North American trade over the next decade on a rolling, year-to-year basis, requiring annual reviews.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: `As it stands` 为方式状语从句，意为“就目前情况而言”；`on a rolling, year-to-year basis` 为介词短语说明运作方式；`requiring annual reviews` 为现在分词短语作结果状语。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 状语从句 + 介词短语说明方式 + 现在分词作结果状语
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 按照目前情况，该条约将在未来十年以逐年滚动续期的方式继续管辖北美贸易，这就需要每年进行审议。

idioms.meaning_zh: 紧跟着……之后；作为……的结果
idioms.usage_note: 常用于描述事件发生后的后果，尤指紧随不愉快或重要事件之后。
idioms.example: In the wake of the scandal, several executives resigned.

idioms.meaning_zh: 一个令人恼火或敏感的问题
idioms.usage_note: 指容易引起不悦或争吵的话题。
idioms.example: His high salary is a sore point among the other employees.

idioms.meaning_zh: 就目前情况来看；按照现状
idioms.usage_note: 用于说明当前状况，尤指尚未发生改变时。
idioms.example: As it stands, the project is behind schedule.

idioms.usage_note: 用于表示数量多于某个数值，通常后接数字。
idioms.example: The repair cost could be upwards of a thousand dollars.

reading_questions.question: What is the main topic of the article?

options.A: The history of US-Canada trade disputes
options.B: The role of the USMCA in North American trade
options.C: Trump's imposition of a 50% tariff on Canadian goods
options.D: The impact of tariffs on global economy

reading_questions.question: According to the text, which of the following products is EXEMPT from the new 50% tariff?
What can be inferred from the fact that Trump's executive orders did not mention wildfires?

options.A: Wildfires are no longer a concern.
options.B: The tariffs were primarily motivated by trade disputes, not environmental issues.
options.C: Trump forgot to include the wildfire issue.

reading_questions.question: In paragraph 2, the word "escalation" most nearly means:
What is the author's attitude toward the tariffs?
How does the author organize the article?

options.B: Problem and solution to trade disputes
options.C: Comparison of US and Canadian tariffs
options.D: Cause and effect of trade tensions

reading_questions.question: Which of the following is the best title for this article?

options.B: Trump Imposes 50% Tariff on Canadian Goods

sources.title: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
